title: It's-a Me, Mario Day!
story: Every March 10, video game fans celebrate National Mario Day because the date sounds like "Mar10" - get it? On this awesome day, Nintendo stores offer special deals on Mario games, and fans share their favorite Mario memories online. Kids dress up as Mario, Luigi, and Princess Peach for school spirit days. Some pizza places even create mushroom pizzas that look like the power-up mushrooms from the games! Gaming cafes host Mario Kart tournaments where players race as their favorite characters. It's the perfect day to dust off your Nintendo console and jump into the Mushroom Kingdom for some classic platforming fun.
activity_question: Why is National Mario Day celebrated on March 10?
activity_choices: Because Mario was created in March
Because mushrooms grow best in March
bonus_fact: Mario has appeared in over 200 video games since his debut!

title: Harriet Tubman: Hero of Freedom Day
story: Every March 10, people across America celebrate Harriet Tubman Day to honor one of history's bravest heroes. Harriet Tubman escaped slavery and then risked her life many times to help other enslaved people find freedom through the Underground Railroad. Today, schools hold special lessons about her courage, libraries display books about her amazing life, and communities organize events to remember her legacy. Many people visit museums or historical sites connected to her story. Social media fills with posts sharing her inspiring quotes and incredible achievements. This day reminds us that one person's bravery can change the world.
activity_question: What was the Underground Railroad that Harriet Tubman used?
activity_choices: A secret network that helped enslaved people escape to freedom
A real train that ran underground through tunnels
A subway system in the southern states
A magical railroad made of rainbow tracks
activity_answer: A secret network that helped enslaved people escape to freedom
bonus_fact: Harriet Tubman never lost a single person on her Underground Railroad missions.

title: Tibet Day: Remembering a Mountain Culture
story: Every March 10, people around the world celebrate Tibet Day to honor Tibetan culture and remember their homeland. Tibetans and supporters gather to share traditional foods like momos (dumplings) and butter tea. They display colorful prayer flags, play folk music, and tell stories about the beautiful mountains of Tibet.
activity_question: What traditional Tibetan food is often shared on Tibet Day?
bonus_fact: Tibetan prayer flags come in five colors that represent different elements of nature.
